h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically apply the food-to-eater rule to correct the remaining arrows on the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Test this mark before it goes on the copy sheet. `hawk -> rabbit` says energy in hawk moves into rabbit; for this feeding, name the food, name the eater, then flip the arrow if the food label comes second.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a new sample card to provide a concrete reference point for the learner's arrow-direction test.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Test this mark before it goes on the copy sheet. `hawk -> rabbit` says energy in hawk moves into rabbit; for this feeding, name the food, name the eater, then flip the arrow if the food label comes second.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a new sample card to provide a concrete reference point for the learner's arrow-direction test.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Test this mark before it goes on the copy sheet. `hawk -> rabbit` says energy in hawk moves into rabbit; for this feeding, name the food, name the eater, then flip the arrow if the food label comes second.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a new sample card to provide a concrete reference point for the learner's arrow-direction test.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: the label is doing real work
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to reject using the same number for two different scale labels.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor physically covers the labels to validate the learner's complaint and demonstrate the necessity of the labels.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No learner pivot occurs, leaving the tutor to proceed with the planned validation of the labels.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No learner pivot occurs, leaving the tutor to proceed with the planned validation of the labels.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: is the rule just side factor squared?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to apply the grid logic to find the area for different side factors.
